high_means: Infection, inflammation, stress, steroids, leukemia, myeloproliferative disorder
low_means: Marrow suppression, sepsis, viral infection, chemo, autoimmune, B12/folate deficiency
pearl: Always interpret with the differential — a normal total WBC can hide a left shift or lymphopenia.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Polycythemia vera, hypoxia/COPD, dehydration (relative), high erythropoietin
low_means: Anemia, blood loss, hemolysis, marrow failure, overhydration (dilutional)
pearl: RBC count alone is misleading; pair with Hgb/Hct and the indices to classify anemia.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Polycythemia, chronic hypoxia, dehydration, EPO excess, high altitude
low_means: Anemia of any cause — blood loss, hemolysis, deficiency, marrow disease
pearl: Hgb is the key anemia metric; transfusion threshold is typically ~7 g/dL (8 in cardiac/CV disease).
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Polycythemia, dehydration, hypoxia, smoking
low_means: Anemia, hemodilution, acute blood loss (lags early)
pearl: Rule of 3: Hct ≈ 3x Hgb. Discordance suggests lab error, in vivo hemolysis, or paraprotein.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Macrocytosis: B12/folate deficiency, alcohol, liver disease, hypothyroid, MDS, reticulocytosis
low_means: Microcytosis: iron deficiency, thalassemia, anemia of chronic disease, sideroblastic
pearl: The master switch for classifying anemia — micro / normo / macrocytic drives the workup.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Macrocytic anemias (tracks with MCV); B12/folate deficiency
low_means: Hypochromia: iron deficiency, thalassemia
pearl: MCH largely parallels MCV; offers little beyond it but flags hypochromia.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Hereditary spherocytosis, cold agglutinins/in vitro hemolysis, lipemia (spurious)
low_means: Iron deficiency, thalassemia (hypochromia)
pearl: MCHC >36 is a red flag for spherocytosis or a spurious result (agglutination/lipemia).
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Anisocytosis: iron deficiency, B12/folate deficiency, mixed anemia, recent transfusion
pearl: High RDW + low MCV favors iron deficiency over thalassemia trait (where RDW is usually normal).
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Reactive thrombocytosis (infection, iron deficiency, post-splenectomy), essential thrombocythemia
low_means: ITP, DIC, TTP/HUS, marrow failure, hypersplenism, drugs, sepsis, pseudothrombocytopenia
pearl: Confirm unexpected low counts with a smear to exclude EDTA-induced platelet clumping.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Increased platelet turnover (ITP, peripheral destruction), large/young platelets
low_means: Marrow hypoproliferation, aplastic anemia, Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome
pearl: High MPV with low platelets points to peripheral destruction rather than production failure.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Bacterial infection, inflammation, stress, steroids, tissue necrosis, CML
low_means: Viral infection, chemo/drugs, severe sepsis, marrow failure, autoimmune
pearl: ANC <500 = severe neutropenia / high infection risk; fever in this setting is an emergency.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Viral infection (EBV, CMV), pertussis, CLL, acute lymphoblastic leukemia
low_means: HIV, steroids, sepsis, chemo/radiation, immunodeficiency, post-viral
pearl: Persistent absolute lymphocytosis in older adults warrants flow cytometry to exclude CLL.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Chronic infection (TB), recovery phase of infection, autoimmune, CMML
low_means: Hairy cell leukemia, aplastic anemia, steroids, acute infection
pearl: Sustained monocytosis >1.0 with cytopenias raises concern for CMML.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Allergy/atopy, parasites, drug reaction, asthma, vasculitis (EGPA), hypereosinophilic syndrome
low_means: Acute stress, steroids, Cushing syndrome (usually clinically insignificant)
pearl: Remember NAACP for eosinophilia: Neoplasm, Allergy, Addison, Connective tissue, Parasites.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Myeloproliferative disorders (CML), hypothyroidism, allergic/inflammatory states
low_means: Acute infection, hyperthyroidism, steroids (rarely significant)
pearl: Unexplained basophilia is a classic clue to a myeloproliferative neoplasm, especially CML.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Appropriate marrow response: hemolysis, acute blood loss, response to therapy
low_means: Hypoproliferative anemia: marrow failure, iron/B12/folate deficiency, renal disease
pearl: Correct for anemia (RPI); distinguishes hyperproliferative from hypoproliferative anemia.
panelName: Complete Blood Count (CBC) + differential + RBC indices

high_means: Hypernatremia: free water deficit/dehydration, diabetes insipidus, excess salt/hypertonic intake
low_means: Hyponatremia: SIADH, heart/liver/renal failure, thiazide diuretics, adrenal insufficiency, primary polydipsia
pearl: Na reflects water balance, not total body salt. Correct measured Na for hyperglycemia: add ~1.6-2.4 mEq/L per 100 mg/dL glucose above 100.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Hyperkalemia: renal failure, acidosis, cell lysis/rhabdo, K-sparing diuretics, ACEi/ARB, hypoaldosteronism
low_means: Hypokalemia: GI/renal losses, diuretics, alkalosis, insulin, beta-agonists, hyperaldosteronism
pearl: Get an ECG for severe derangement (peaked T waves vs U waves). Pseudohyperkalemia from hemolysis or fist-clenching is common; redraw if unexpected.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Hyperchloremia: normal saline infusion, non-anion-gap metabolic acidosis (RTA, diarrhea), dehydration
low_means: Hypochloremia: vomiting/NG suction, metabolic alkalosis, loop/thiazide diuretics
pearl: Usually tracks Na. A Cl shift discordant from Na points to an acid-base disturbance; needed to calculate the anion gap.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Metabolic alkalosis (vomiting, diuretics, hyperaldosteronism) or renal compensation for chronic respiratory acidosis
low_means: Metabolic acidosis (DKA, lactic, uremia, diarrhea) or renal compensation for respiratory alkalosis
pearl: Surrogate for plasma HCO3 (venous, not ABG). A low value should prompt anion gap calculation: Na - (Cl + HCO3), normal 8-12.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Hyperglycemia: diabetes, stress/illness, steroids, pancreatitis; fasting >=126 (x2) diagnostic of DM
low_means: Hypoglycemia: insulin/sulfonylurea excess, sepsis, adrenal/liver failure, alcohol; symptoms typically <70
pearl: Fasting 100-125 = prediabetes (impaired fasting glucose). Falsely low if drawn in a non-fluoride tube due to ongoing glycolysis; use a gray-top fluoride/oxalate tube.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Renal failure, prerenal azotemia (dehydration), GI bleed, high protein intake, catabolic/steroid states
low_means: Liver failure, malnutrition/low protein, overhydration, pregnancy, SIADH
pearl: BUN/Cr ratio >20:1 suggests prerenal cause or GI bleed; ratio ~10-15:1 suggests an intrinsic renal cause.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Decreased GFR: AKI, CKD, obstruction, dehydration; also high muscle mass and certain drugs (raise without true GFR drop)
low_means: Low muscle mass, cachexia/malnutrition, advanced liver disease, pregnancy (hemodilution + increased GFR)
pearl: Insensitive early marker - rises only after substantial GFR loss, and roughly doubles per halving of GFR. Use eGFR and trend the value, not a single point.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Hypercalcemia: primary hyperparathyroidism, malignancy (PTHrP/bone mets), thiazides, vitamin D excess, sarcoidosis
low_means: Hypocalcemia: hypoparathyroidism, vitamin D deficiency, CKD, hypomagnesemia, pancreatitis, hypoalbuminemia (low total only)
pearl: Correct for albumin: add 0.8 mg/dL per 1 g/dL albumin below 4.0. Check ionized Ca (the true active fraction) when in doubt.
panelName: Basic Metabolic Panel (electrolytes, glucose, BUN, creatinine, Ca)

high_means: Hepatocellular injury; also muscle damage, MI, hemolysis; very high (>1000) in viral, ischemic, or toxic hepatitis
low_means: Not clinically significant; rarely low in vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) deficiency or chronic hemodialysis
pearl: AST:ALT ratio >2:1 suggests alcoholic liver disease (mnemonic: a 'toAST' with Scotch). Found in liver, heart, and muscle, so less liver-specific than ALT.
panelName: Liver function tests & pancreatic enzymes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin, lipase)

high_means: Most liver-specific marker of hepatocellular injury (viral hepatitis, NAFLD, drugs/toxins, ischemia)
low_means: Not clinically significant; may reflect vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) deficiency
pearl: More liver-specific than AST. ALT>AST favors viral hepatitis or NAFLD; very high values (>25x ULN, often thousands) point to ischemic or toxic (acetaminophen) injury.
panelName: Liver function tests & pancreatic enzymes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin, lipase)

high_means: Cholestasis/biliary obstruction, infiltrative liver disease; also bone disease (Paget's, mets, healing fracture), pregnancy, childhood growth
low_means: Hypophosphatasia, zinc or magnesium deficiency, hypothyroidism, malnutrition, Wilson disease
pearl: Confirm hepatic source with a concurrently elevated GGT; if GGT is normal, elevated ALP is likely bony or physiologic in origin.
panelName: Liver function tests & pancreatic enzymes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin, lipase)

high_means: Hemolysis or Gilbert syndrome (unconjugated/indirect); biliary obstruction, hepatitis, cholestasis (conjugated/direct)
low_means: Not clinically significant
pearl: Fractionate: indirect predominant (direct <20% of total) = hemolysis/Gilbert; direct elevation (>20% of total or >2 mg/dL) = hepatobiliary disease. Scleral icterus appears ~2-3 mg/dL.
panelName: Liver function tests & pancreatic enzymes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin, lipase)

high_means: Almost always dehydration (hemoconcentration); no true pathologic cause of overproduction
low_means: Chronic liver disease, nephrotic syndrome, malnutrition, inflammation (negative acute-phase reactant), protein-losing enteropathy
pearl: Marker of hepatic synthetic function with a long half-life (~20 days), so it reflects chronic rather than acute liver injury; low albumin is a poor prognostic sign.
panelName: Liver function tests & pancreatic enzymes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin, lipase)

high_means: Acute pancreatitis (>3x ULN is diagnostic); also pancreatic duct obstruction, renal failure, bowel ischemia/obstruction
low_means: Not clinically significant; may reflect advanced pancreatic exocrine insufficiency
pearl: More sensitive and specific than amylase for pancreatitis, with longer elevation (up to 8-14 days). Magnitude does NOT correlate with disease severity.
panelName: Liver function tests & pancreatic enzymes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin, lipase)

high_means: Elevated ASCVD risk; familial hypercholesterolemia, hypothyroidism, nephrotic syndrome, cholestasis
low_means: Malnutrition, hyperthyroidism, severe liver disease, malabsorption, chronic illness/inflammation
pearl: A single number masks the LDL/HDL split — always interpret alongside the full lipid breakdown, not in isolation.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

range: <100 optimal; 100-129 near/above optimal; 130-159 borderline high; 160-189 high; ≥190 very high
high_means: Primary atherogenic driver; raises ASCVD risk; suspect FH if ≥190; statin candidate
low_means: Usually favorable; very low may reflect malnutrition, liver disease, hyperthyroidism, or abetalipoproteinemia
pearl: Friedewald-calculated LDL is invalid when triglycerides >400 mg/dL — order direct LDL or use non-HDL instead.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

high_means: Generally cardioprotective; ≥60 counts as a negative (favorable) risk factor
low_means: Independent ASCVD risk factor; seen in metabolic syndrome, smoking, sedentary lifestyle, high triglycerides
pearl: Very high HDL (>90-100) is not necessarily protective and may signal dysfunctional HDL or CETP deficiency — not always 'good.'
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

range: <150 normal; 150-199 borderline high; 200-499 high; ≥500 very high
high_means: Metabolic syndrome, DM, alcohol, obesity, hypothyroidism; ≥500 raises acute pancreatitis risk
low_means: Malnutrition, malabsorption, hyperthyroidism; rarely clinically significant
pearl: Best measured after a 9-12h fast; non-fasting elevations are common — recheck fasting before acting on a borderline value.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

range: Goal <130 (= Total − HDL); typically ~30 mg/dL above the LDL goal
high_means: Captures all atherogenic (apoB-containing) particles; better risk predictor than LDL when triglycerides are elevated
low_means: Lower atherogenic particle burden; generally favorable
pearl: Remains valid when non-fasting or TG >400 — the preferred secondary target when calculated LDL is unreliable.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

range: Below sex-specific 99th-percentile URL (assay-dependent); hs assays report in ng/L
high_means: Myocardial injury: MI, myocarditis, type 2 MI, PE, sepsis, renal failure, HF, takotsubo
pearl: The rising/falling delta plus clinical context defines acute MI — a single elevation marks injury, not necessarily infarction.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

high_means: Myocardial injury/MI; also skeletal muscle damage, rhabdomyolysis, myocarditis, cardiac surgery
pearl: Largely supplanted by troponin; its faster clearance (rises 3-6h, normalizes ~48-72h) makes it useful mainly for detecting reinfarction.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

range: <100 HF unlikely; 100-400 indeterminate; >400 HF likely (acute dyspnea)
high_means: Ventricular wall stress: heart failure, fluid overload, PE, renal failure; rises with age and female sex
low_means: HF unlikely; strong negative predictive value in acute dyspnea
pearl: Falsely low in obesity; sacubitril/valsartan (ARNI) raises BNP since it is a neprilysin substrate — use NT-proBNP to monitor those patients.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

high_means: Heart failure, age, renal dysfunction, AF, pulmonary hypertension, sepsis
low_means: HF unlikely; excellent negative predictive value
pearl: Not a neprilysin substrate, so unaffected by ARNI therapy — the preferred natriuretic peptide for monitoring on sacubitril/valsartan.
panelName: Lipid panel & cardiac markers (troponin, BNP, CK-MB, cholesterol)

high_means: Extrinsic/common pathway defect: warfarin, vitamin K deficiency, liver disease, DIC, factor VII deficiency
low_means: Generally not clinically significant; may reflect hypercoagulable/acute-phase state
pearl: PT probes the extrinsic pathway (factor VII) — the first factor to fall in liver disease/vit K deficiency due to its short half-life.
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Anticoagulation effect (warfarin), liver synthetic dysfunction, vitamin K deficiency, DIC, factor deficiency
low_means: Subtherapeutic warfarin; possible relative hypercoagulability
pearl: INR standardizes PT across reagents via the ISI — monitors warfarin, not DOACs or heparin. Mechanical mitral valves target 2.5-3.5.
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Intrinsic pathway defect: heparin, hemophilia A/B, vWD, lupus anticoagulant, factor XII/XI deficiency, DIC
low_means: Acute-phase elevation of factor VIII; sampling/hypercoagulable artifact
pearl: Monitors unfractionated heparin. Mixing study distinguishes factor deficiency (corrects) from an inhibitor like lupus anticoagulant (does not correct).
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Active fibrinolysis: VTE (DVT/PE), DIC, recent surgery, trauma, pregnancy, malignancy, sepsis, advancing age
pearl: High sensitivity, low specificity — best as a rule-out at low/intermediate pretest probability. Use age-adjusted cutoff (age x 10 ng/mL) over 50; positive needs imaging.
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Acute-phase reactant: inflammation, infection, malignancy, pregnancy, trauma (also raises ESR)
low_means: Consumption (DIC), liver failure, massive transfusion/dilution, congenital afibrinogenemia, thrombolytic therapy
pearl: Falling fibrinogen in DIC is an ominous late sign. Replace with cryoprecipitate; transfuse when <100 mg/dL (<150-200 in obstetric/active bleeding).
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Reactive thrombocytosis (infection, iron deficiency, inflammation, post-splenectomy) or essential thrombocythemia
low_means: Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP/HUS, DIC, heparin-induced (HIT), sepsis, bone marrow failure, hypersplenism, drugs
pearl: Spontaneous bleeding risk rises sharply <10,000-20,000; check a smear to exclude EDTA clumping (pseudothrombocytopenia) before workup.
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Platelet dysfunction (aspirin/NSAIDs, uremia), von Willebrand disease, thrombocytopenia, connective tissue disorders
pearl: Largely obsolete and poorly reproducible — replaced by platelet function assays (PFA-100/closure time) and vWF studies for evaluating primary hemostasis.
panelName: Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT, D-dimer, fibrinogen, platelets, bleeding time)

high_means: Alkalemia — respiratory alkalosis (hyperventilation) or metabolic alkalosis (vomiting, diuretics, hyperaldosteronism)
low_means: Acidemia — metabolic acidosis (DKA, lactic, renal failure) or respiratory acidosis (hypoventilation)
pearl: Interpret pH first to define acidemia vs alkalemia, then PaCO2 vs HCO3 for the primary disorder; check compensation against expected formulas.
panelName: Arterial blood gas & acid-base (pH, PaCO2, PaO2, HCO3, anion gap, lactate)

high_means: Hypercapnia — respiratory acidosis (COPD, sedation, OHS) or appropriate compensation for metabolic alkalosis
low_means: Hypocapnia — respiratory alkalosis (anxiety, pain, PE, sepsis) or compensation for metabolic acidosis
pearl: Respiratory parameter: in primary respiratory disorders PaCO2 moves opposite to pH; in metabolic compensation it moves the same direction as HCO3.
panelName: Arterial blood gas & acid-base (pH, PaCO2, PaO2, HCO3, anion gap, lactate)

high_means: Hyperoxia — usually supplemental O2; rarely clinically harmful short-term
low_means: Hypoxemia — V/Q mismatch, shunt, hypoventilation, low FiO2/high altitude, diffusion impairment
pearl: Use A-a gradient (normal ≈ age/4 + 4) to separate hypoventilation/low FiO2 (normal gradient) from shunt/V-Q mismatch/diffusion defect (widened).
panelName: Arterial blood gas & acid-base (pH, PaCO2, PaO2, HCO3, anion gap, lactate)

high_means: Metabolic alkalosis (vomiting, diuretics, hyperaldosteronism) or renal compensation for respiratory acidosis
low_means: Metabolic acidosis (lactic, DKA, diarrhea, RTA) or renal compensation for respiratory alkalosis
pearl: ABG HCO3 is calculated via Henderson-Hasselbalch; compare with measured total CO2 on a chemistry panel and investigate large discordance.
panelName: Arterial blood gas & acid-base (pH, PaCO2, PaO2, HCO3, anion gap, lactate)

high_means: High-AG metabolic acidosis — GOLD MARK / MUDPILES (lactate, ketones, uremia, toxic alcohols, salicylates)
low_means: Low AG — hypoalbuminemia, paraproteinemia (myeloma), lithium, bromide; or lab artifact
pearl: AG = Na - (Cl + HCO3); correct upward ~2.5 per 1 g/dL albumin below 4, and check delta-delta for concurrent mixed disorders.
panelName: Arterial blood gas & acid-base (pH, PaCO2, PaO2, HCO3, anion gap, lactate)

high_means: Type A (hypoperfusion/shock, sepsis, ischemia) or Type B (metformin, liver failure, malignancy, thiamine deficiency, toxins)
low_means: Not clinically significant
pearl: Lactate >4 mmol/L flags severe sepsis/shock and guides resuscitation; lactate clearance trend predicts outcome better than a single value.
panelName: Arterial blood gas & acid-base (pH, PaCO2, PaO2, HCO3, anion gap, lactate)

high_means: Primary hypothyroidism; also recovery phase of nonthyroidal illness or poor levothyroxine adherence
low_means: Hyperthyroidism (Graves, toxic nodule); central hypothyroidism; over-replacement with levothyroxine
pearl: Most sensitive single test for thyroid function; check first. TSH + free T4 together localize the lesion (primary vs central).
panelName: Endocrine labs (TSH, free T4, HbA1c, cortisol, PTH, insulin)

high_means: Hyperthyroidism; levothyroxine over-replacement; thyroiditis releasing stored hormone
low_means: Hypothyroidism (primary if TSH high, central if TSH low/normal)
pearl: Prefer free T4 over total T4 - unaffected by TBG shifts (pregnancy, estrogen, nephrotic syndrome). Low free T4 with low/normal TSH = central hypothyroidism.
panelName: Endocrine labs (TSH, free T4, HbA1c, cortisol, PTH, insulin)

high_means: Hyperglycemia over ~3 months; diabetes or poor glycemic control
low_means: Recent hypoglycemia; conditions shortening RBC lifespan (hemolysis, recent transfusion, EPO therapy)
pearl: Reflects ~3 month average glucose (RBC lifespan). Falsely high in iron-deficiency anemia; falsely low in hemolysis/blood loss. Unreliable with hemoglobinopathies.
panelName: Endocrine labs (TSH, free T4, HbA1c, cortisol, PTH, insulin)

high_means: Cushing syndrome; acute stress/illness; exogenous steroids; failure to suppress on dexamethasone
low_means: Adrenal insufficiency (primary/Addison or secondary); AM cortisol <3 strongly suggests deficiency
pearl: Time-dependent - draw at 8 AM (diurnal peak). Random levels are uninterpretable. Confirm excess with low-dose dex suppression; deficiency with cosyntropin (ACTH) stimulation.
panelName: Endocrine labs (TSH, free T4, HbA1c, cortisol, PTH, insulin)

high_means: Primary hyperparathyroidism (with high Ca); secondary (with low/normal Ca in CKD, vit D deficiency)
low_means: Hypoparathyroidism; hypercalcemia of malignancy; high calcium/vitamin D intake suppressing PTH
pearl: Always interpret with a simultaneous serum calcium. High Ca + high/inappropriately normal PTH = primary hyperparathyroidism; high Ca + low PTH = malignancy.
panelName: Endocrine labs (TSH, free T4, HbA1c, cortisol, PTH, insulin)

high_means: Insulin resistance/metabolic syndrome; insulinoma; sulfonylurea use; exogenous insulin
low_means: Type 1 diabetes; advanced beta-cell failure in long-standing type 2
pearl: Interpret with simultaneous glucose. Insulinoma = high insulin + high C-peptide during documented hypoglycemia; surreptitious insulin = high insulin with LOW C-peptide.
panelName: Endocrine labs (TSH, free T4, HbA1c, cortisol, PTH, insulin)

high_means: Concentrated urine: dehydration/volume depletion, SIADH; falsely high with glucosuria, proteinuria, IV contrast (osmotic solutes)
low_means: Dilute urine: water overload/polydipsia, diabetes insipidus, diuretics, tubular concentrating defect
pearl: Fixed SG ~1.010 (isosthenuria) = lost concentrating ability—think CKD/tubular injury. Heavy glucose, protein, or radiocontrast falsely raise it; refractometer corrects better than dipstick.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

range: Negative to trace (normal <150 mg/24h; albumin <30 mg/24h)
high_means: Glomerular disease, diabetic/hypertensive nephropathy, UTI, CHF; benign orthostatic, febrile, or exercise proteinuria
pearl: Dipstick detects mainly albumin—misses Bence Jones light chains; send urine protein electrophoresis if myeloma suspected. Quantify/confirm with UPCR or 24h collection.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

high_means: Hyperglycemia exceeding renal threshold (DM); or normoglycemic glucosuria (pregnancy, SGLT2 inhibitors, Fanconi syndrome)
pearl: Glucosuria with normal serum glucose = proximal tubular defect (Fanconi) or SGLT2 inhibitor. Glucose oxidase dipstick is glucose-specific; high vitamin C can cause false negatives.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

high_means: DKA, starvation/fasting, low-carb (ketogenic) diet, alcoholic ketoacidosis, pregnancy, vomiting/febrile illness in children
pearl: Nitroprusside dipstick detects acetoacetate (and acetone), NOT beta-hydroxybutyrate—the dominant ketone in early DKA; can underestimate severity and paradoxically rise during recovery as BHB converts to AcAc.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

high_means: Hematuria: stones, UTI, malignancy, glomerulonephritis, trauma, BPH; or pigment—hemoglobinuria (hemolysis), myoglobinuria (rhabdomyolysis)
pearl: Positive dipstick with NO RBCs on micro = hemoglobinuria or myoglobinuria. Dysmorphic RBCs/RBC casts → glomerular source; isomorphic RBCs → urologic/lower tract.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

high_means: Pyuria: UTI, urethritis/STI, interstitial nephritis (AIN), appendicitis adjacent to bladder, contamination; sterile pyuria (TB, chlamydia, treated UTI)
pearl: Marker of WBCs (even lysed); ~78–90% sensitive for UTI. Pair with nitrite: both positive strongly supports UTI; LE+/nitrite− still suggests infection. High glucose/protein or vitamin C can blunt it.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

high_means: Infection by nitrate-reducing Gram-negative rods: E. coli, Klebsiella, Proteus, Enterobacter, Serratia
pearl: Highly specific but poorly sensitive—negative doesn't exclude UTI. Misses non-reducers (Enterococcus, Staph saprophyticus, Pseudomonas) and needs urine to dwell >4h in bladder; first-morning sample best.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

range: Negative to occasional hyaline casts (normal, esp. after exercise/dehydration)
high_means: RBC casts=glomerulonephritis; WBC casts=pyelonephritis/AIN; muddy-brown granular=ATN; waxy/broad=advanced CKD; fatty=nephrotic syndrome
pearl: Casts form in renal tubules, so they localize disease to the kidney. RBC casts = glomerulonephritis until proven otherwise; muddy-brown granular casts = ATN, the classic intrinsic-AKI clue.
panelName: Urinalysis (specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, leukocyte esterase, nitrites, casts)

range: 10-18 (100-180 mm H2O); upper normal up to 20-25 in obese adults
high_means: Bacterial/TB/fungal (esp. cryptococcal) meningitis, mass lesion, venous sinus thrombosis, IIH, cerebral edema
low_means: CSF leak, dehydration, prior LP/overdrainage, complete spinal subarachnoid block
pearl: Measure in lateral decubitus with legs extended (relaxed). Very high in cryptococcal meningitis; serial/therapeutic LPs reduce ICP and mortality.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: Any meningitis/encephalitis; hundreds-thousands with neutrophils suggests bacterial
pearl: Correct traumatic tap: subtract ~1 WBC per 500-700 RBCs (if normal blood counts). Bacterial often >1000; viral/TB/fungal usually <500 (lymphocytic).
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

range: 0 (no PMNs normally; rare single PMN can be normal)
high_means: Neutrophil predominance = bacterial meningitis (also early viral, very early TB, amebic)
pearl: Lymphocytic predominance points to viral, TB, or fungal. Early viral may be PMN-heavy; repeat LP at 12-24h shifts toward lymphocytes.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: Highest in bacterial/TB (often >100-500, TB can exceed 1000); moderate in fungal; mildly elevated in viral
low_means: Rare; CSF leak/hypovolemia, water intoxication, very rapid CSF turnover
pearl: TB classically very high protein with a cobweb/pellicle (fibrin) clot on standing. Albuminocytologic dissociation (high protein, normal cells) = GBS, not meningitis.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: Reflects systemic hyperglycemia; not diagnostically useful in meningitis
low_means: Low (hypoglycorrhachia) in bacterial, TB, fungal; typically normal in viral
pearl: Always pair with a serum glucose drawn ~30-60 min before LP; CSF glucose normally lags serum changes. Markedly low favors bacterial/TB/fungal.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: Normal/high ratio favors viral (aseptic) meningitis
low_means: <0.4 highly suggestive of bacterial, TB, or fungal meningitis
pearl: More reliable than absolute CSF glucose because it corrects for serum level. Ratio falls (not rises) with hyperglycemia, so check it rather than absolute value in diabetics.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: >=3.5 mmol/L supports bacterial meningitis; also elevated in TB/fungal
pearl: Distinguishes bacterial from viral, esp. useful post-neurosurgery; less confounded by serum glucose than the glucose ratio. Normal lactate favors viral. Prior antibiotics lower sensitivity.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: Positive identifies bacteria; 60-90% sensitive if untreated, falls after antibiotic exposure
pearl: Yield scales with organism load: high for pneumococcus/meningococcus, lower (~1/3) for Listeria. A negative Gram stain does NOT exclude bacterial meningitis.
panelName: CSF analysis (opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose) across meningitis types

high_means: Iron overload (hemochromatosis); also acute-phase reactant: infection, inflammation, malignancy, liver injury
low_means: Iron deficiency (most specific test); depleted iron stores
pearl: Low ferritin is the single most specific marker of iron deficiency. But it's an acute-phase reactant, so inflammation can falsely normalize/elevate it (consider deficiency if <100 with inflammation).
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Iron overload, hemolysis, excess intake/transfusion, liver disease
low_means: Iron deficiency, anemia of chronic disease, acute inflammation
pearl: Marked diurnal variation (peaks AM) and fluctuates with diet/recent intake; interpret with TIBC as transferrin saturation, not alone.
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Iron deficiency (upregulated transferrin), pregnancy, OCP/estrogen use
low_means: Anemia of chronic disease/inflammation, malnutrition, liver disease, nephrotic syndrome
pearl: TIBC is a surrogate for transferrin. High TIBC + low ferritin = iron deficiency; low/normal TIBC + high/normal ferritin = anemia of chronic disease.
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: >45% triggers hereditary hemochromatosis screening; >60% (M)/>50% (F) highly specific for iron overload
low_means: <20% suggests iron deficiency or anemia of chronic disease
pearl: TSAT = (serum iron / TIBC) x 100. Best single index to distinguish iron deficiency (low) from overload (high).
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Iron deficiency, pregnancy, estrogen/OCP
low_means: Anemia of chronic disease, malnutrition, liver disease, protein loss (nephrotic)
pearl: Negative acute-phase reactant - falls with inflammation. Often reported alongside TIBC (TIBC mcg/dL approx = transferrin mg/dL x 1.4).
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Liver disease, myeloproliferative disorders, recent supplementation; rarely clinically significant
low_means: Pernicious anemia, malabsorption, vegan diet, metformin/PPI use; causes macrocytic anemia + neuropathy
pearl: Borderline-low B12 (200-300): check methylmalonic acid (elevated in true deficiency). Neuro damage can precede anemia and be irreversible.
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Supplementation, recent intake; not clinically significant
low_means: Dietary deficiency, alcoholism, malabsorption, pregnancy, methotrexate; macrocytic anemia
pearl: Always check B12 before treating folate deficiency - folate alone corrects anemia but lets B12 neuro damage progress. RBC folate reflects stores better than serum.
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Acute inflammation/infection, tissue injury, malignancy; >100 suggests serious bacterial infection
low_means: — (normal; no clinical low)
pearl: Rises within 6-8h, peaks ~48h - faster and more dynamic than ESR. hs-CRP (different assay) used for cardiovascular risk stratification. Beware mg/dL vs mg/L (x10).
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Inflammation, infection, malignancy, anemia; very high (>100) = GCA/PMR, myeloma, abscess, osteomyelitis
low_means: Polycythemia, sickle cell, microcytosis, hyperviscosity (rarely significant)
pearl: Nonspecific and slow to change. Age-adjusted upper limit: age/2 (men), (age+10)/2 (women). Key in temporal arteritis (GCA) workup.
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)

high_means: Gout, tumor lysis, renal failure, thiazides/loop diuretics, high purine intake, psoriasis
low_means: SIADH, Fanconi syndrome, allopurinol, high-dose salicylates; usually benign
pearl: Can be normal or even low during an acute gout flare - don't exclude gout on a single normal level; joint aspiration for monosodium urate crystals is definitive.
panelName: Iron studies & misc (ferritin, TIBC, transferrin, B12/folate, CRP/ESR, uric acid)
